name: validate-all: detects two source files declaring the same rule id (cross-file collision rejected at validate-all stage, before aggregator)
command:
mkdir -p tree/google tree/owasp && for ns in google owasp; do printf 'source:
  id: %s-src
  authoritative_publisher: %s
  authoritative_url: "https://%s.com"
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
  content_hash: "sha256:S"
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - id: g-shared-001
    name: shared-rule
    description: detects something across collisions
    detector: rubric/detectors/g-shared-001.sh
    type: problem
    fixable: null
    has_suggestions: false
    deprecated: false
    replaced_by: []
    docs_url: "https://x.com"
    requires_type_checking: false
    recommended: true
    severity: P1
    version: 1.0.0
    semver: 1.0.0
    rule_state: warn-only
    provenance:
      - source: x
        class: published-standard
        section_id: x
        url: "https://x.com"
        fetched_at: "2026-05-04T00:00:00Z"
        content_hash: "sha256:S"
recommended_set: [g-shared-001]
all_set: [g-shared-001]
' "$ns" "$ns" "$ns" > "tree/$ns/x.yaml"; done && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards/validate-all.sh" --root "$PWD/tree" --check-cross-file-collisions
